Victor, I missed the leadership call this morning. Can you debrief me on what happened?
Of course, Elena. Let me cut to the chase. There were three main topics. I will outline them for you.
First, the CFO mentioned that they were cutting the LATAM marketing budget by fifteen percent. She confirmed it would take effect next quarter.
Fifteen percent? Did anyone push back? Was there any consensus on that?
Not really. James told me he had already submitted the headcount report, so that part is done. But he warned that the board would not approve any new hires this quarter.
That is frustrating. What about the growth targets? Any flexibility there?
Elena suggested they might revisit the targets if Q2 numbers improved. The director acknowledged that the current goals were ambitious but insisted we should proceed with the original plan for now.
Got it. Can you circulate the meeting notes so I can clarify a few things with the Buenos Aires team? We need to bring about change in how we communicate these directives.
He mentioned they would escalate the issue to the VP if no consensus was reached.
She confirmed the mandate was final and all regions had to comply.
She confirmed that the budget would be cut by fifteen percent.

I will circulate the meeting notes so we can reach a consensus.
Let me outline the key decisions and we can proceed from there.

Good morning everyone. This is Karen Liu, Chief Operating Officer, with a brief update on our Q1 priorities. After reviewing the year-end results, the executive team has reached a consensus on three key directives. First, we are going to proceed with the LATAM expansion plan that Victor's team outlined last quarter. The board confirmed that the budget allocation would increase by twelve percent. Second, all regional leads must circulate their updated growth forecasts by January fifteenth. Leadership insisted that these forecasts include headcount projections and conversion rate targets. Third, we are restructuring the digital platform team. The CTO mentioned that he had already begun the hiring process, and he suggested that each region commit at least two senior engineers to the project. I want to clarify one important point: the restructuring does not mean budget cuts. The CFO acknowledged that some teams were concerned about this, but she confirmed that all current budgets would remain intact. Finally, I want to debrief on the client escalation from last week. The client warned us that if we did not improve delivery timelines, they might consider other providers. We need to bring about change in our operations workflow immediately. That is all for now. Please confirm your attendance for next Thursday's follow-up. Thank you.

First, state who the meeting was with and when it took place. Second, report the main decisions that were made. Third, mention any concerns or objections that were raised. Fourth, outline the action items and deadlines. Fifth, confirm the next steps and who is responsible for each.
